name: UX design process (5 stages)
description: Define→research→prototype→test→iterate. Includes what PMs should provide to design at each stage to reduce kickoff churn.

metadata.intent: Run a lightweight UX process that produces decisions you can defend (not just screens)

must_use: User needs + behavior evidence early (even lightweight)
must_not: Skip testing for high-risk flows when a small prototype test is feasible
conventions: Iterate based on observed behavior, not purely stakeholder preference.

steps.description: Write a clear problem statement: user segment + context + pain + desired outcome. Include constraints and non-goals.
Collect behavioral evidence: interviews, surveys, usability tests, support tickets, analytics. Use AI summarization carefully but always validate with raw examples.
If helpful, define lightweight personas / jobs-to-be-done to align the team on who is served and what tradeoffs are acceptable.
Prototype the flow before pixel perfection. A rough wireflow can reveal missing states, unclear choices, and brittle assumptions early.
Run small usability tests and observe where users hesitate, fail, or misunderstand. Prefer 'show me' over 'tell me'.
After launch, monitor activation/conversion/support signals; refine flows. Design is not done at launch—instrumentation + iteration keeps UX aligned with reality.
